message.content:
You are the Prepare Agent. You are given a list of papers, searching results of the papers on GitHub. Your task is to choose at least 5 repositories as the reference codebases. Note that this time there is no innovative ideas, you should choose the most valuable repositories as the reference codebases.

## List of papers:

1. SkillRL: Skill-Based Reinforcement Learning for LLM Agents with Automatic Skill Discovery (arXiv:2602.08234)
   - Core framework for automatic skill abstraction and hierarchical skill library construction from agent experiences

2. ToolLLM: Facilitating Large Language Models to Master 16000+ Real-world APIs (arXiv:2307.16789, ICLR'24 Spotlight)
   - Established framework for LLM tool-use with large-scale API integration, neural API retriever, and evaluation methodology

3. AgentSkillOS: Leveraging, Managing, and Scaling the Agent Skill Ecosystem
   - Operating system for discovering, composing, and running skill pipelines from 90K+ skills using skill trees and DAG orchestration

4. SkillsBench: Evaluating How Agent Skills Improve AI Agent Performance
   - First benchmark for evaluating agent skill effectiveness across 11 domains, showing +51.9pp improvement in healthcare domain

## Searching results of the papers on GitHub:

=== Paper 1: SkillRL ===
    Name: aiming-lab/SkillRL
    Description: SkillRL: Evolving Agents via Recursive Skill-Augmented Reinforcement Learning
    Link: https://github.com/aiming-lab/SkillRL
    Stars: 276

******************************

=== Paper 2: ToolLLM ===
    Name: OpenBMB/ToolBench
    Description: [ICLR'24 spotlight] An open platform for training, serving, and evaluating large language model for tool learning.
    Link: https://github.com/OpenBMB/ToolBench
    Stars: 5524

    Name: THUNLP-MT/StableToolBench
    Description: A new tool learning benchmark aiming at well-balanced stability and reality, based on ToolBench.
    Link: https://github.com/THUNLP-MT/StableToolBench
    Stars: 214

******************************

=== Paper 3: AgentSkillOS ===
    Name: ynulihao/AgentSkillOS
    Description: Build your agent from 90,000+ skills via skill RETRIEVAL & ORCHESTRATION
    Link: https://github.com/ynulihao/AgentSkillOS
    Stars: 128

******************************

=== Paper 4: SkillsBench ===
    (No direct GitHub repository found for this paper)

******************************

=== Additional keyword searches: Medical domain repos ===
    Name: bowang-lab/MedSAM
    Description: Segment Anything in Medical Images
    Link: https://github.com/bowang-lab/MedSAM
    Stars: 4071

    Name: bowang-lab/MedSAM2
    Description: MedSAM2: Segment Anything in 3D Medical Images and Videos
    Link: https://github.com/bowang-lab/MedSAM2
    Stars: 542

    Name: CUHK-AIM-Group/MedSAM-Agent
    Description: MedSAM-Agent: Empowering Interactive Medical Image Segmentation with Multi-turn Agentic Reinforcement Learning
    Link: https://github.com/CUHK-AIM-Group/MedSAM-Agent
    Stars: 30

    Name: Stanford-AIMI/CheXagent
    Description: [Arxiv-2024] CheXagent: Towards a Foundation Model for Chest X-Ray Interpretation
    Link: https://github.com/Stanford-AIMI/CheXagent
    Stars: 213

    Name: Joey-S-Liu/MedSAM3
    Description: MedSAM3: Delving into Segment Anything with Medical Concepts
    Link: https://github.com/Joey-S-Liu/MedSAM3
    Stars: 204

    Name: bowang-lab/MedSAMSlicer
    Description: 3D Slicer Plugin for Segment anything in medical images
    Link: https://github.com/bowang-lab/MedSAMSlicer
    Stars: 281

    Name: The-Swarm-Corporation/MRI-Swarm
    Description: MRI Swarm is an enterprise-grade system for coordinating specialized medical imaging agents
    Link: https://github.com/The-Swarm-Corporation/MRI-Swarm
    Stars: 3

    Name: jdh-algo/Citrus-V
    Description: Citrus-V: Advancing Medical Foundation Models with Unified Medical Image Grounding for Clinical Reasoning
    Link: https://github.com/jdh-algo/Citrus-V
    Stars: 18

    Name: AI-in-Health/M3FM
    Description: [npj Digital Medicine] A multimodal multidomain multilingual medical foundation model for zero shot clinical diagnosis
    Link: https://github.com/AI-in-Health/M3FM
    Stars: 17

## Task context:
The research project aims to build a Medical Skills library and Agent framework for medical image computing and computer-assisted intervention tasks. The framework will: (1) collect and encapsulate existing medical AI tools as standardized Skills; (2) design new skill modules; (3) build an LLM-powered Medical Agent that can automatically discover, select, and compose skills for complex multi-step clinical workflows. This targets MICCAI 2026 with the theme "From Algorithms to Clinical Translation."

## Your output format:
Return a JSON object with:
- "reference_codebases": list of repo names (owner/repo format) you selected
- "reference_paths": list of where they would be cloned (Experiment/code_references/repo_name)
- "reference_papers": list of paper titles that should be downloaded from arXiv
- "reasoning": brief explanation of why each codebase was selected

Choose the MOST VALUABLE repos that cover: (a) agent skill frameworks, (b) medical imaging tools that could become skills, (c) medical agent systems. Aim for at least 7 repos to ensure comprehensive coverage.

content.text: I'll analyze the available repositories and select the most valuable ones for building a Medical Skills library and Agent framework. Let me first examine the project structure to understand the context better.

    37→  "task2": "Build a Medical Skills library and Agent framework for medical image computing and computer-assisted intervention tasks, targeting MICCAI 2026. The project aims to: (1) Collect and curate existing open-source medical AI models and tools (e.g., segmentation models like MedSAM, classification models, detection models, registration models, report generation models) and encapsulate them as standardized, callable Medical Skills; (2) Design new skill modules to fill gaps in the medical skill ecosystem; (3) Build a Medical Agent powered by a large language model that can automatically discover, select, and compose appropriate Medical Skills to solve complex, multi-step medical tasks (e.g., a clinical workflow involving image preprocessing, organ segmentation, lesion detection, measurement, and report generation). The framework should support hierarchical skill organization, intelligent skill retrieval based on task requirements, and dynamic skill composition via execution graphs. The key innovation lies in bridging the gap between general-purpose agent skill frameworks (like ToolLLM, AgentSkillOS, SkillRL) and the specialized requirements of the medical imaging domain, where skills require domain-specific validation, safety constraints, and multi-modal data handling. The project should align with MICCAI 2026's theme 'From Algorithms to Clinical Translation' by demonstrating practical clinical utility.",

Specific datasets to be determined during the idea generation and code survey phases. Candidate public datasets include: medical image segmentation benchmarks (BTCV, AMOS, TotalSegmentator), classification benchmarks (CheXpert, ISIC), VQA benchmarks (VQA-RAD, PathVQA), and clinical report generation benchmarks (MIMIC-CXR, IU X-Ray). The evaluation should measure both individual skill performance and the agent's ability to compose skills for complex multi-step clinical workflows."

content.text:
Now I have a complete picture. Let me make my selections and produce the output. Let me write the result as a JSON file to the appropriate location.

Based on my thorough analysis of the project context, the source papers, the GitHub search results, and the project goals (building a Medical Skills library and Agent framework for MICCAI 2026), here is my selection:

```json
{
  "reference_codebases": [
    "aiming-lab/SkillRL",
    "OpenBMB/ToolBench",
    "ynulihao/AgentSkillOS",
    "bowang-lab/MedSAM",
    "bowang-lab/MedSAM2",
    "CUHK-AIM-Group/MedSAM-Agent",
    "Stanford-AIMI/CheXagent",
    "THUNLP-MT/StableToolBench"
  ],
  "reference_paths": [
    "Experiment/code_references/SkillRL",
    "Experiment/code_references/ToolBench",
    "Experiment/code_references/AgentSkillOS",
    "Experiment/code_references/MedSAM",
    "Experiment/code_references/MedSAM2",
    "Experiment/code_references/MedSAM-Agent",
    "Experiment/code_references/CheXagent",
    "Experiment/code_references/StableToolBench"
  ],
  "reference_papers": [
    "SkillRL: Skill-Based Reinforcement Learning for LLM Agents with Automatic Skill Discovery",
    "ToolLLM: Facilitating Large Language Models to Master 16000+ Real-world APIs",
    "AgentSkillOS: Leveraging, Managing, and Scaling the Agent Skill Ecosystem",
    "SkillsBench: Evaluating How Agent Skills Improve AI Agent Performance"
  ],
  "reasoning": {
    "aiming-lab/SkillRL": "Core methodological reference (Paper 1). Provides the foundational architecture for automatic skill discovery and hierarchical skill library construction via RL. Its recursive skill-augmented approach directly informs how Medical Skills can be automatically discovered, abstracted, and organized from agent experiences. 276 stars indicates solid community validation.",
    "OpenBMB/ToolBench": "Primary tool-use framework reference (Paper 2, ICLR'24 Spotlight). With 5524 stars, this is the most mature and well-established codebase for LLM tool integration. Its architecture for API registration, neural tool retrieval (ToolRetriever), and decision tree reasoning (DFSDT) provides the blueprint for the Medical Agent's skill retrieval and composition pipeline. The standardized tool API format is directly adaptable for encapsulating medical AI tools as callable skills.",
    "ynulihao/AgentSkillOS": "Skill ecosystem management reference (Paper 3). Its skill tree organization, DAG-based pipeline orchestration, and retrieval system for 90K+ skills provide the architectural template for managing a large-scale Medical Skills library. The skill composition and pipeline execution concepts are directly applicable to multi-step clinical workflows (e.g., preprocessing -> segmentation -> detection -> reporting).",
    "bowang-lab/MedSAM": "Premier medical image segmentation foundation model (4071 stars). This is the single most important medical tool to encapsulate as a Medical Skill. MedSAM's prompt-based segmentation architecture is a natural fit for agent-driven skill invocation. Its codebase demonstrates how to handle medical image I/O, preprocessing, and model inference -- patterns that will be replicated across many Medical Skills.",
    "bowang-lab/MedSAM2": "Next-generation medical segmentation extending MedSAM to 3D images and videos (542 stars). Critical for the project because many clinical workflows involve volumetric data (CT, MRI). Provides the 3D/video segmentation skill that complements MedSAM's 2D capabilities, and demonstrates how to handle temporal/volumetric medical data -- a key requirement for MICCAI-relevant clinical translation.",
    "CUHK-AIM-Group/MedSAM-Agent": "The most directly relevant repository -- it is the intersection of medical segmentation and agentic RL. It demonstrates how to build an agent that interacts with MedSAM via multi-turn reinforcement learning. This provides a concrete template for how the Medical Agent can invoke segmentation skills with iterative refinement, a pattern essential for clinical-grade segmentation accuracy.",
    "Stanford-AIMI/CheXagent": "Medical foundation model for chest X-ray interpretation from Stanford AIMI (213 stars). Provides a complementary medical skill beyond segmentation: clinical report generation and image interpretation. Its architecture for multi-modal medical reasoning (image understanding + text generation) is a key skill type needed for the end-to-end clinical workflow the project envisions. Demonstrates medical VQA and report generation capabilities.",
    "THUNLP-MT/StableToolBench": "Improved evaluation framework building on ToolBench (214 stars). Addresses stability and reproducibility issues in tool-use evaluation -- critical concerns for medical AI where evaluation reliability is paramount. Its balanced evaluation methodology will inform how the Medical Skills framework measures agent performance on clinical workflows. Complements ToolBench with more robust benchmarking practices."
  }
}
```

## Summary of Selection Rationale

The 8 selected repositories provide comprehensive coverage across the three required dimensions:

### (a) Agent Skill Frameworks (4 repos)
- **SkillRL** -- Skill discovery and hierarchical organization via RL
- **ToolBench** -- Large-scale tool/API integration, retrieval, and LLM-based reasoning
- **AgentSkillOS** -- Skill ecosystem management, DAG orchestration, and pipeline composition
- **StableToolBench** -- Stable evaluation methodology for tool-use agents

### (b) Medical Imaging Tools to Become Skills (3 repos)
- **MedSAM** -- 2D medical image segmentation (the foundational segmentation skill)
- **MedSAM2** -- 3D medical image and video segmentation (volumetric clinical workflows)
- **CheXagent** -- Chest X-ray interpretation and report generation (diagnostic reasoning skill)

### (c) Medical Agent Systems (1 repo)
- **MedSAM-Agent** -- The direct bridge between medical segmentation and agentic systems, demonstrating multi-turn agentic interaction with medical tools

### Repos NOT Selected and Why
- **MedSAM3, MedSAMSlicer**: Redundant with MedSAM/MedSAM2; MedSAM3 adds medical concept integration but less architectural novelty, MedSAMSlicer is a UI plugin rather than a framework
- **MRI-Swarm**: Only 3 stars, limited maturity and documentation
- **Citrus-V, M3FM**: Low star counts (18, 17) and narrower scope; less architectural value compared to selected repos
- **SkillsBench**: No GitHub repository found; the paper will still be referenced

### Papers for Download
All 4 source papers are included for download since they provide the theoretical foundations even when code is unavailable (SkillsBench).
